page: Microsoft Azure D4as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and a 33 GB local storage drive with support for Premium Storage. The processor is equipped with 32 MB of L3 cache and supports hardware-accelerated instructions such as AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark results indicate a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench is comparatively weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads scoring in the lower tier. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as Passmark CPU encryption and single-threaded gzip compression, perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4as_v4 offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads that do not demand sustained multi-core execution or high memory throughput. Given its strong single-core performance but weaker multi-core scaling and database operation metrics, this instance is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded application hosting, basic text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by a 4-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, a 33 GB local storage volume, and is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng tests, though multi-core scaling, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower performance tier. Single-threaded compression and cryptographic tasks deliver average throughput. This hardware configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that prioritize single-core execution over parallel processing, making it suitable for lightweight web serving, text processing, and general-purpose development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 16 GB of RAM, and 33 GB of local storage. It offers strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for basic workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
Equipped with 33 GB of local storage and Premium Storage capability
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and development tasks
Weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth relative to resource density
